words.tip: Soft 'g'. The vegetable aisle. Singular: de groente.
'ui' vowel. Het fruit = fruit as a category. Individual fruits mostly take 'de'.
Long 'ay' at end. Found in most Belgian supermarkets — fresh bread daily.
het-word. 'Ik eet geen vlees' = I don't eat meat.
Short 'i'. 'De visafdeling' = the fish counter.
'ui' vowel. The dairy section: milk, cheese, yoghurt, butter.
Plural of 'de drank'. The drinks aisle.
Niet (not) + voeding (food). Toiletries, cleaning products, etc.
het-word! Long 'oo'. 'Een sneetje brood' = a slice of bread.
'Een pak melk' = a carton of milk. Very common purchase!
Long 'aa'. Belgium is famous for its cheese. 'Een stuk kaas' = a piece of cheese.
het ei (singular) → de eieren (plural). Common mistake: never 'de ei'!
'Een pakje boter' = a pack of butter. -er ending reduces to schwa: [BOH-tuh].
Soft 'g'. 'Een potje yoghurt' = a small pot of yoghurt.
Long 'oo'. Not to be confused with 'de look' (garlic)! 'Slagroom' = whipped cream.
Short 'a'. 'Een sneetje ham' = a slice of ham.
Short 'a'. 'Een stuk zalm' = a piece of salmon.
'ij' = 'eye' sound. 'Een blik tonijn' = a tin of tuna.
Classic Belgian dish! Mosselen met frietjes. Note: 'mosselen' not 'mossels'.
het-word. Belgium has 1500+ beers. 'Een fles bier' = a bottle of beer.
'ij' = 'eye'. 'Een fles wijn' = a bottle of wine.
het-word. 'Een brik fruitsap' = a carton of fruit juice.
Uncountable as a substance. 'Een reep chocolade' = a bar of chocolate.
Belgian Dutch! NL Dutch uses 'jam'. 'Een pot confituur' = a jar of jam.
Belgian Dutch for garlic! NL Dutch: 'knoflook'. 'Een teentje look' = a clove of garlic.
Diminutive plural of 'koek'. 'Een doos koekjes' = a box of biscuits.
Tand (tooth) + pasta. 'Een tube tandpasta' = a tube of toothpaste.
Long 'ee'. 'Een stuk zeep' = a bar of soap.
English loanword. 'Een fles shampoo' = a bottle of shampoo.
het-word. Wassen (to wash) + poeder (powder). 'Een pak waspoeder' = a pack of washing powder.
het-word. Afwassen (to wash up) + middel (product). 'Een fles afwasmiddel'.
